name: Thrud
description: Thrud (Þrúðr) is a Norse goddess, the daughter of Thor, the god of thunder, and Sif, the golden-haired goddess. Her name translates to "strength" or "might," reflecting her inherent power and association with physical prowess. While not as extensively featured in surviving Norse texts as some other deities, Thrud's existence is confirmed in the Eddas, primarily in the Prose Edda of Snorri Sturluson. She is primarily known through mentions related to a planned marriage to the dwarf Alviss, who desired her hand. This betrothal was ultimately thwarted by Thor, who, through a clever contest of wits, tricked Alviss into staying up all night until he was turned to stone by the rising sun. This tale showcases Thor's protectiveness of his daughter and highlights the potential dangers of alliances between gods and dwarves. Beyond this central myth, details about Thrud's specific powers or responsibilities are scarce, but her lineage strongly suggests an association with strength, battle, and the protection of her family and Asgard. Some interpretations connect her to the Valkyries, suggesting a role in choosing who lives or dies on the battlefield, although this is not explicitly stated in the primary sources. Her existence reinforces the importance of family and the dangers of deception within the Norse pantheon, even if her individual role remains somewhat enigmatic compared to her more prominent parents. She likely possessed considerable strength and resilience, inherited from her father. Her story serves as a reminder of the complexities and interconnectedness of the Norse gods and the constant potential for conflict and intrigue.
subtitle: Daughter of Thor, Goddess of Strength

keyMyths.title: The Marriage of Thrud
keyMyths.description: Thor prevents the dwarf Alviss from marrying Thrud by tricking him into staying up until sunrise, which turns Alviss to stone. This highlights Thor's protectiveness and cunning.

keyMyths.title: Thrud's Lineage
keyMyths.description: As the daughter of Thor and Sif, Thrud inherits strength and beauty from her divine parents. This lineage contributes to her status and potential roles within Asgard.

keyMyths.title: Potential Valkyrie Association
keyMyths.description: While not explicitly stated, some interpretations suggest Thrud's potential association with the Valkyries, due to her connection to strength and battle. This remains speculative.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: Primary source for Norse mythology, including mentions of Thrud and the story of her planned marriage.
sources.text: Primary source for Norse mythology, including mentions of Thrud and the story of her planned marriage.

sources.author: H.R. Ellis Davidson
